    "Mixed old and new belts installed on a multi-groove sheave/pulley drive.",
    "Uneven belt tension and load distribution across grooves due to differing belt lengths, wear, and elasticity.",
    "Accelerated wear on new belts and sheave grooves; risk of belt slippage, heat buildup, and premature failure.",
    "Non-compliance with manufacturer belt replacement guidance for multi-groove drives."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop equipment and replace all belts in the multi-groove set with a matched set from the same manufacturer/lot.",
    "Inspect sheaves for groove wear, scoring, or mismatch; replace or resurface if necessary.",
    "Check and adjust belt tension per manufacturer specifications.",
    "Document replacement and establish PM practice to replace all belts in a set together."
  ],
  "rationale": "Installing mixed old and new belts on a multi-groove drive violates manufacturer-recommended practice. Differences in belt length, wear, and stiffness cause uneven load sharing, leading to rapid degradation, slippage, heat, and potential failure while in service. The correct repair is to replace the entire belt set as a matched group.",
  "references": [
    "mfr guides"
  ]
}
